steps.title: Welcome to Pulsar
steps.description:
Welcome! This tutorial will show you Pulsar's two modes in action with Excel.

You will:
1. Press `Ctrl+Q` to open Switch Mode and switch to Excel
2. Press `Ctrl+Shift+Q` to open Command Mode and run a VBA demo in Excel

You'll see how to switch to an app, then do something inside it.

steps.title: Switch Mode: app switching and launching
steps.description:
Press `Ctrl+Q` to open Switch Mode, hover over the Excel icon, then release the hotkey to switch to it.

That's all it takes — Pulsar jumps straight to Excel. No alt-tabbing, no searching.
steps.waitHintText: Press Ctrl+Q, hover over the Excel icon, and release the hotkey.

steps.title: Switched to Excel
steps.description:
Great — you switched to Excel!

In the next step you'll use Command Mode to run a VBA demo in Excel.

Click "Next" to continue.

steps.title: Command Mode: run an action
steps.description:
Press `Ctrl+Shift+Q` to open Command Mode, hover over "Run VBA Demo", then release the hotkey.

Pulsar will execute a VBA script in Excel for you — that's Command Mode in action: running a plugin on the app you switched to.
steps.waitHintText: Press Ctrl+Shift+Q, hover over "Run VBA Demo", and release the hotkey.

steps.title: Command executed!
steps.description:
Pulsar ran the VBA script in Excel! You just combined both modes:

1. Switch Mode (`Ctrl+Q`) — got you to Excel
2. Command Mode (`Ctrl+Shift+Q`) — ran a VBA demo inside it

That's the core Pulsar workflow.

steps.title: You're all set!
steps.description:
You just combined both Pulsar modes:

✔ Switched to Excel with Switch Mode (`Ctrl+Q`)
✔ Ran a VBA demo with Command Mode (`Ctrl+Shift+Q`)

That's the core workflow: switch to any app, then act inside it.

Open Settings anytime to customize your slots, or keep using the defaults — Pulsar is ready whenever you are!
